entries.title: Stocks Close Out a Strong May
entries.description: Plus, Dell soars and workers get less of the pie.

entries.title: Common myths about gut health
entries.description: Marketers sell all kinds of tests and supplements to fix your gut health. But is any of this actually worth spending money on?

entries.title: Oil Declines Lift Stocks to Fresh Records
entries.description: Brent crude fell 19% in May, its sharpest monthly drop since 2020.

entries.title: Federal judge blocks Trump from officially renaming Kennedy Center
entries.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked President Trump from officially renaming the Kennedy Center. U.S. District Judge Christopher Cooper found the board did not have the authority to rename the facility on its own.

entries.title: Tracking the Ebola outbreak: Where the deadly virus is spreading and how the U.S. is responding
entries.description: Learn more about the deadly 2026 Ebola outbreak, including how many cases there are so far, which countries are most affected, how this outbreak compares with others in severity and more.

entries.title: Coders are refusing to work without AI — and that could come back to bite them
entries.description: While AI is helping coders produce code faster, it may not be producing better code, researchers warn. And that could cause problems down the road for them.

entries.title: SpaceX awarded $6.45B in Space Force contracts ahead of IPO
entries.description: SpaceX already generated one-fifth of its 2025 revenue from government contracts, the company revealed in its IPO filing.

entries.title: Blue Origin rocket explosion could delay NASA's moon plans
entries.description: A major setback for Blue Origin and potentially for NASA, after one of the company's New Glenn rockets exploded last night. No one was hurt. Blue Origin is set to play a key role in NASA's push to go back to the moon, but now that launch schedule is in question. NBC's Tom Costello reports.

entries.title: Poor sleep linked to rising cancer risk in under-50s
entries.description: Findings add to growing efforts to explain why cancer rates are increasing among younger adults worldwidePoor sleep may be fuelling the global rise in under-50s being diagnosed with cancer, two large studies suggest.The number of younger people diagnosed with the disease has risen by almost 80% in three decades. Worldwide cases of early-onset cancer increased from 1.82m in 1990 to 3.26m in 2019, while cancer deaths among people in their 40s, 30s or younger rose by 27%. Continue reading...

entries.title: Lead federal prosecutor in James Comey seashells photo case steps aside
entries.description: Federal prosecutor in seashells case against FBI Director James Comey has resigned.

entries.title: Quote of the Day — John Eliot
entries.description: “Confidence is not a guarantee of success, but a pattern of thinking that will improve your likelihood of success, a tenacious search for ways to make things work.” — John Eliot

entries.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entries.description: Franklin D. Roosevelt was the first president to be televised, in ceremonies opening the New York World's Fair in April 1939.

entries.title: Life Hack of the Day
entries.description: Use hair straighteners to iron collars.

entries.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entries.description:
Don’t pursue happiness – create it.

Lucky Numbers: 3, 68, 83, 67, 28, 13

entries.title: One birthday, two party planners: Freedom 250 vs. America250, explained
entries.description: Learn more about how the America250 and Freedom 250 events differ as artists, including Martina McBride, Young MC and The Commodores, back out of Trump concert.

entries.title: ICE eyes selling mega-warehouses purchased for mass detention
entries.description: WASHINGTON — The Trump administration is looking into selling some of the large warehouses that Immigration and Customs Enforcement had purchased earlier this year to serve as mega-detention centers for immigrants, according to two Department of Homeland Security officials

entries.title: I went to the so-called ‘steroid Olympics,’ to understand why Silicon Valley is obsessed with peptides
entries.description: The Enhanced Games — a singular sporting competition where a majority of the athletes were on performance enhancing drugs — may herald a new business model that the tech industry is ready to embrace.

entries.title: The U.N. Is Going Broke as the U.S. and China Withhold Billions
entries.description: The Trump administration has failed to pay billions of dollars owed to the international body, while Beijing has been accused of gaming the budget.

entries.title: Ships Are Sailing ‘Dark’ to Sneak Out of Strait of Hormuz
entries.description: A tiny flow of vessels, including some large oil and gas tankers, are making the dangerous transit with help from the U.S. military, in some cases turning off lights and automatic navigation systems.

entries.title: UFC Macau video: Rei Tsuruya's rear-naked choke taps out Luis Gurule
entries.description: Rei Tsuruya rebounded in impressive fashion at UFC Fight Night 277.

entries.title: Does Caffeine Work Differently For Women? What New Research Shows
entries.description: And how to use it to improve athletic performance.

entries.title: Karren Brady breaks silence on West Ham United’s relegation
entries.description: Former West Ham United vice-chairman Karren Brady has reacted to the Hammers’ relegation to the Championship. The London Stadium club will be playing in the second division next term and have decide...

entries.title: Report: Julian Alvarez agent to meet with Atletico Madrid after Barcelona trolling
entries.description: Atletico Madrid took to social media to react to what they have described as a smear campaign against the club and star forward Julian Alvarez. After months of rumours surrounding the future of Alvare...

entries.title: Tennis world reacts after Spanish star Rafael Jodar denies pushing ball girl at French Open
entries.description: Jodar progressed to the fourth round of the French Open for the first time on Friday, but immediately faced questions after viral footage showed a ball girl losing balance as the tennis star crossed her path

entries.title: Canavan stars as Tyrone beat Derry to retain Ulster Minor title
entries.description: Conan Canavan scores 1-4 as Tyrone power past Derry with a 2-16 to 1-12 win in Letterkenny to retain the Ulster Minor title.

entries.title: Official: Jonas Boldt installed as sporting director of Hannover 96
entries.description: Jonas Boldt will take over the position of Managing Director of Sport at Hannover 96 on 1 June 2026, the club has announced.Having received a contract until summer of 2029, Boldt will be responsible f...

entries.title: Commanders insider reveals what stood out during first week of OTAs
entries.description: Something stood out on the Commanders' first day of OTAs.

entries.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Needs More From Asian Allies to Secure Balance Against China
entries.description: The defense secretary adopted a softer tone on Beijing in a speech to partners concerned about the U.S.’s security commitment.
